skill.name: gemini-api-dev
skill.description: Guides developers building Gemini API integrations with current SDKs, model selection, multimodal inputs, function calling, structured outputs, and official documentation lookup.
skill.summary: Build Gemini API integrations with SDK examples, model guidance, and official documentation links.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk weak-crypto and command-execution patterns, but these are false positives from markdown text, code examples, and inline backticks. The confirmed risk is that the skill instructs agents to fetch official Google API discovery and documentation URLs, which is legitimate for API development but requires network access awareness.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed External Documentation Fetching
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to fetch Google API discovery and documentation URLs. The domains appear official and relevant, but network access from a community skill can expose metadata and should remain transparent to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs agents to fetch external Google documentation and discovery endpoints. The purpose is legitimate, but the network behavior is explicit and user-visible.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography at lines 3 and 10, but those lines describe Gemini API capabilities. No cryptographic algorithm use, implementation, or recommendation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose in YAML front matter and overview text. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or another weak algorithm being used.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged As Commands
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged many inline backticks and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are markdown code spans and SDK examples, not executable skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown documentation with fenced Python, TypeScript, and Go snippets. There is no script file or instruction that executes these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Import Block Flagged As Dynamic Import
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged a dynamic import expression, but the cited lines are a Go import block inside a documentation example. No dynamic JavaScript import is used by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are a static Go import list in a fenced code block. I found no evidence of JavaScript dynamic import execution.

low_findings.title: Benign System Capability Mention
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged system reconnaissance at line 14, but the line describes structured output. No host, environment, or filesystem reconnaissance instruction appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 14 is a bullet about generating valid JSON matching a schema. I found no evidence of system discovery or local information gathering.

dangerous_patterns.title: External URL Fetch Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to retrieve live API documentation from official Google endpoints. This is useful for current API work, but it is still external network activity.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and fetch instructions are explicit. They point to official documentation, so the concern is controlled network access rather than malicious intent.

content.user_title: Build Gemini API Apps with Current SDK Guidance
content.value_statement: Gemini API integrations change quickly, and stale examples can cause broken implementations. This skill provides SDK guidance, model selection help, and official documentation lookup steps.
actual_capabilities: Shows quick-start examples for Python, TypeScript, and Go Gemini SDKs.
Guides model selection for reasoning, speed, multimodal work, and image generation.
Points agents to official Gemini REST discovery specifications.
Links to documentation for function calling, structured outputs, embeddings, and media understanding.
Warns users away from legacy SDK names and older model families.
limitations: The skill is documentation guidance, not a working Gemini client library.
Model names and API details may change after the skill was authored.
It requires network access to verify the latest official documentation.
It does not manage API keys, billing settings, or Google Cloud project setup.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Gemini Chat Feature
use_cases.description: Use the SDK examples and model guidance to start a basic text generation integration.

use_cases.title: Add Structured Model Outputs
use_cases.description: Find the official structured output guidance before designing schemas for downstream application logic.

use_cases.title: Plan a Multimodal Workflow
use_cases.description: Review links for image understanding, media input, function calling, and embeddings before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Gemini Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me start a Gemini API project in Python. Include the current SDK package and a minimal request flow.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Gemini Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare Gemini model options for a low-latency multimodal assistant. Explain the tradeoffs in practical terms.

prompt_templates.title: Design Structured Outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to guide a Gemini structured output design for extracting fields from support tickets. Include validation considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Debug API Integration Drift
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to verify current Gemini REST API fields from official documentation and identify likely causes of an integration failure.

output_examples.input: I need to build a Gemini text generation endpoint in TypeScript.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering SDK package choice, client initialization, model selection, request shape, and response handling.

output_examples.input: I want Gemini to return structured data for product reviews.
output_examples.output: A schema planning checklist with field design, validation steps, model behavior notes, and links to official structured output guidance.

output_examples.input: My Gemini integration may use outdated model names.
output_examples.output: A migration review that identifies legacy SDK or model references and recommends checking the official docs index.

best_practices: Verify model names and request fields against official Gemini documentation before implementation.
Keep API keys outside prompts, logs, source control, and shared examples.
Use structured outputs when application logic depends on predictable response fields.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on old Gemini SDK package names without checking migration guidance.
Do not paste private API keys or user data into troubleshooting prompts.
Do not assume documentation links are current if network verification is unavailable.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps plan and implement Gemini API integrations using SDK examples, model guidance, and official documentation links.

faq.question: Does it call the Gemini API for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples, but your agent or application performs any API calls.

faq.question: Why does it include external URLs?
faq.answer: The URLs point to official Google documentation and discovery specs for current API details.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk notice because it instructs agents to fetch external documentation.

faq.question: Can it help with function calling?
faq.answer: Yes. It links to the official function calling documentation and frames when to use tool-style model behavior.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
